title: Versioned substrate documents never converge their persisted schema_version at write time — config is the live instance: reads migrate in memory forever, writes persist the stale version back
description: Read-time migration (loadConfigForDir, TASK-070) carries a lagging config forward in memory only; no write path stamps the current schema version, so the staleness is permanent: writeConfigForDir (context.ts:872-874) passes schema_version through verbatim, every sanctioned mutation writes 1.0.0 back (observed: amendConfigEntryForDir on a live-copy — on-disk 1.0.0 before load, after chain-walked load, and after the write), every load resolves the chain forever, and future bumps stack hops on every read. Fresh substrates are born lagging: writeSkeletonConfig writes a literal "1.0.0" (context.ts:948) and adoptConception writes conception.json's "1.0.0" against the 1.7.0 bundled schema. Scope hole confirmed against filed text: TASK-070 criterion 3 pins "carried forward in memory" (read half only); TASK-073 criterion 2 stamps "the whole-block object ... on every block write" (blocks only); no FEAT-010 criterion states persisted config version converges. Class: read-time in-memory migration without write-time version convergence — blocks (remedy filed, TASK-073, planned), config (this instance, unfiled until now), migrations.json (latent sibling: appendMigrationDeclForDir carries the loaded envelope version forward verbatim, migrations-store.ts:208-219, and loadMigrationsFileForDir has no migration path; benign while file and schema both sit at 1.0.0).

evidence.reference: writeConfigForDir delegates to writeTypedFile with the caller's object verbatim — nothing anywhere sets schema_version; the AJV gate validates but does not stamp.

Observed write-path fact (scratch byte-copy of the live substrate): amendConfigEntryForDir load→mutate→write persisted schema_version 1.0.0 unchanged after a chain-walked load — the sanctioned write writes the stale version back.

writeSkeletonConfig writes a literal schema_version "1.0.0"; adoptConception writes samples/conception.json which itself declares "1.0.0" (line 2) against the bundled 1.7.0 schema — fresh substrates are born in the mismatch branch.

Latent class sibling: appendMigrationDeclForDir carries the loaded migrations.json envelope schema_version forward verbatim, and loadMigrationsFileForDir raw-validates with no migration path — reproduces this defect at the first migrations-schema bump.

runMigrations applies chain fns only; an identity decl returns data unchanged, so even the in-memory form keeps 1.0.0 — the chain is resolved on every load, permanently (loadConfigForDir consults the registry on every mismatch).

impact: The on-disk validity signal is permanently false (1.0.0 declared, 1.7.0 effective) — raw readers cannot distinguish stale from current; chain resolution is paid on every config read forever and grows with every future bump; the ceremony-seeding and recovery machinery becomes load-bearing steady state for a fact one write-time stamp would make durable; fresh catalog substrates begin life in the mismatch branch.
proposed_resolution: Stamp schema_version = the bundled config schema version inside writeConfigForDir before delegating to writeTypedFile — truthful by construction (the AJV gate validates the post-stamp object against the bundled schema; every mutating caller loads through the migrating loader first), symmetric with TASK-073's block-envelope design, and self-converging (the first sanctioned write persists the current version; subsequent loads version-match and never consult the registry). Also fixes born-lagging fresh substrates (skeleton + adoptConception writes stamp current). Optional in-memory hygiene: replace the identity decl with a declarative set of schema_version (mechanically supported today). migrations.json's latent sibling is named for the class, addressed when its schema first bumps or folded into TASK-073's implementation locus if the stamp lands generically in writeTypedFile. Determination at task time.
